_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A2/005 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Ben; computer plays Emma. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 005 · Healthy Habits

routes.meaning: Ben thanks Emma and says he eats healthy food now.
examples: Thanks! I eat healthy food now.
Thank you! I eat healthy food now.
Thanks! I eat healthy food these days.
Thanks a lot! I eat healthy food now.
routes.hint: 💬 Reply to Emma: “Thanks! I eat healthy food now.”

routes.meaning: Ben thanks Emma and says he feels healthy.
examples: Thank you! I feel healthy now.
Thanks! I feel really good.
Thank you! I take care of myself now.
Thanks! I live a healthy life now.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “Thanks! I eat healthy food now.”

partial.message: Reply to Emma: “Thanks! I eat healthy food now.”

off_topic.message: Emma gave you a compliment. Try “Thanks! I eat healthy food now.”

routes.meaning: Ben says he eats vegetables and fruit every day.
examples: I eat vegetables and fruit every day.
I eat vegetables and fruit each day.
I eat lots of vegetables and fruit every day.
Every day I eat vegetables and fruit.
routes.hint: 💬 Say your food: “I eat vegetables and fruit every day.”

examples: I eat a lot of vegetables.
I eat fruit and vegetables.
I eat healthy meals with vegetables.
Mostly vegetables and fresh fruit.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “I eat vegetables and fruit every day.”

partial.message: Tell Emma what you eat: “I eat vegetables and fruit every day.”

off_topic.message: Emma asked what you eat. Try “I eat vegetables and fruit every day.”

routes.meaning: Ben says no; he drinks water because soda is bad for him.
examples: No, I drink water because soda is bad for me.
No. I drink water because soda is bad for me.
No, I drink water. Soda is bad for me.
No, I only drink water because soda is bad for me.
routes.hint: 💬 Answer with a reason: “No, I drink water because soda is bad for me.”

routes.meaning: Ben says he only drinks water.
examples: No, I only drink water.
No, I just drink water now.
No, I drink water instead.
No soda, only water for me.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “No, I drink water because soda is bad for me.”

routes.meaning: Ben admits he drinks a little soda sometimes.
examples: Sometimes, but not much.
A little, but not often.
Only a little sometimes.
Not much, just a little.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “No, I drink water because soda is bad for me.”

partial.message: Answer Emma: “No, I drink water because soda is bad for me.”

off_topic.message: Emma asked about soda. Try “No, I drink water because soda is bad for me.”

npc.text: Water is better for you. Do you drink water too?

routes.meaning: Ben says yes, he drinks water because soda is bad for him.
examples: Yes, I drink water because soda is bad for me.
Yes, I drink water. Soda is bad for me.
Yes, I drink lots of water.
Yes, water is best because soda is bad for me.
routes.hint: 💬 Agree with a reason: “Yes, I drink water because soda is bad for me.”

partial.message: Tell Emma: “Yes, I drink water because soda is bad for me.”

off_topic.message: Emma asked about water. Try “Yes, I drink water because soda is bad for me.”

routes.meaning: Ben says he also runs every morning.
examples: I also run every morning.
I also run every morning before school.
I run every morning too.
And I also run every morning.
routes.hint: 💬 Add a habit: “I also run every morning.”

routes.meaning: Ben thanks Emma and adds that he runs every morning.
examples: Thanks! I also run every morning.
Thank you! And I run every morning.
Thanks! I run every morning too.
Thank you! I also go running each morning.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “I also run every morning.”

partial.message: Add another habit: “I also run every morning.”

off_topic.message: Tell Emma more. Try “I also run every morning.”

routes.meaning: Ben says because exercise makes him strong.
examples: Because exercise makes me strong.
Because exercise makes me strong and healthy.
Because running makes me strong.
Because exercise keeps me strong.
routes.hint: 💬 Give your reason: “Because exercise makes me strong.”

routes.meaning: Ben says he wants to be strong and healthy.
examples: Because I want to be strong and healthy.
Because I want to stay strong.
Because exercise is good for me.
Because I want to be fit and strong.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “Because exercise makes me strong.”

partial.message: Tell Emma why: “Because exercise makes me strong.”

off_topic.message: Emma asked why you run. Try “Because exercise makes me strong.”

npc.text: That's wonderful, Ben! I want to be healthy too. Keep it up!
